title: Enclave · Compute That Can't See Your Data
excerpt: Attested GPU enclaves for everyone, not just the Fortune 500. Deploy a WebAssembly app straight from your browser, pay per second in ETH or USDC, and verify the enclave yourself before sending a byte.
content_html:
Trustless, attested enclaves · API v1

The hardware that used to hide behind enterprise sales calls is now one click away: deploy a WebAssembly app onto a confidential enclave equipped with a flagship NVIDIA GPU, straight from your browser. Pay per second in ETH or USDC. Your wallet is your account. Then check the cryptographic attestation yourself before you send a single byte of data.

flagship NVIDIA GPUs · confidential CPU + GPU per-tenant Wasm sandbox + process isolation VRAM zeroed before reuse CORS-friendly

enclave · sev-snp + nvidia-cc

launch measurement · live fetching …

The model

Don't trust the operator. Measure it.

Enclave runs your WebAssembly app inside a hardware-isolated, attested enclave with a confidential GPU wired into it, so the operator can't read your memory, your keys, or your traffic, and you don't have to take our word for it. Every claim on this page is something you can check on-chain or in a signed attestation. We're equally explicit about the part that isn't hardware-enforced: how tenants are kept apart from each other on a shared GPU. See exactly how isolation works ↓

verifiable

Trustless

Each enclave produces a CPU attestation report (AMD SEV-SNP on today's fleet) and an NVIDIA GPU attestation report. Verify them client-side against the measurements published on EnclaveHost/enclave releases, then connect.

wallet-native

Crypto-native

Your wallet is your account and nothing is custodied: every payment forwards in the same transaction - to Enclave, and for paid apps the publisher's share straight to their wallet; the contract never holds funds. It buys prepaid runtime, burned per second while your deployment runs. Payments are final: unspent time survives a stop but can't be withdrawn, so fund small and top up as you go.

cors

Browser-native

The whole API is CORS-enabled. Spin up a server, stream logs, and pull attestation from front-end JavaScript. No backend required.

gpu

Real GPUs

Whole flagship NVIDIA GPUs in confidential-computing mode, wired into your Wasm app through a GPU interface. Deploying is two dials - a GPU share and a CPU share - floored at the minimums the app's declared specs imply. How tenants share a card →

Lifecycle

Five calls, start to finish.

The path from an empty wallet to a verified GPU endpoint. Click a step to see exactly what crosses the wire.

Root of trust

What "trustless" actually means here.

"Trustless" isn't a slogan. It's a chain of measurements that ends in your browser. Walk each link to see what gets verified and why the operator can't fake it.

  1. The CPU (AMD SEV-SNP on today's fleet) and the NVIDIA GPU hold keys fused at manufacture. Enclave can't extract or forge them. The silicon vendor (AMD or NVIDIA, Intel on a TDX host) is the root of trust, not the operator.

  2. On boot the AMD secure processor signs a report over the launch measurement: a SHA-384 digest binding the firmware, the kernel, and the exact runtime image. Change one byte and it changes. Your app is deliberately not in this boot measurement: it arrives later by CID, and the measured runtime refuses bytes that don't hash to the CID in your on-chain deployment record. The attestation reports the app's CID and states exactly what covers it; it never pretends the boot quote does. (An Intel TDX host tells the same story via a DCAP quote over MRTD + RTMRs.)

  3. With confidential computing on, the GPU signs an attestation report over your nonce, proving CC mode, driver, and vBIOS before any data reaches VRAM. Check it against NVIDIA's NRAS or nvtrust.

  4. The enclave generates its TLS keypair in sealed memory and folds the public-key hash into the quote's report_data. The private key never leaves the boundary, so the operator can't sit in the middle.

  5. Tinfoil's open-source verifier runs the whole chain client-side: hardware report against the vendor root, Sigstore provenance of the release, measurement comparison, TLS binding. The API's verification.selfCheck is the enclave running these same steps on itself: a labeled diagnostic, because trust only counts when YOUR client computes it. Try it live below.

GET /v1/deployments/{id}/attestation

Don't take this page's word for it: run the check now

Your browser loads Tinfoil's open-source verifier (@tinfoilsh/verifier) and fetches the attestation of one live enclave - the fleet's gateway nominates an active one, and the verdict names exactly which - over your own connection. It validates the hardware report up to the silicon vendor's root of trust (AMD SEV-SNP on today's fleet; the steps below name the technology the enclave actually presented), verifies the release's Sigstore code provenance against the public EnclaveHost/enclave repo, and compares the two measurements. Exactly what the API's verify field tells every client to do - and what your dashboard re-runs against the specific enclave each deployment lands on.

The verifier module is served from this site itself (same-origin, version-pinned), not a third-party CDN; the Sigstore provenance bundles and the silicon vendor's certificate collateral (AMD KDS) it checks come through Tinfoil's CORS proxies. Fully independent path: tinfoil attestation verify -e <enclave-host> -r EnclaveHost/enclave, via tinfoil-cli - swap in any enclave's host to put the rest of the fleet through the same check.

How isolation is achieved

Two boundaries. One is hardware. One is not. We tell you which.

Most "confidential GPU" pitches blur these together. Enclave keeps them separate because they're enforced by different things and carry different guarantees. Here is precisely what protects you, and from whom.

hardware · attested

You vs. the operator

Your enclave runs in a CPU TEE (AMD SEV-SNP on today's fleet) with the GPU in NVIDIA confidential-computing mode. The CPU↔GPU link is encrypted and the GPU is walled off from the host. Enclave, the operator, cannot read your VRAM, RAM, keys, or traffic. This is enforced by hardware and proven by an attestation you verify in your browser before sending data. This is the strong guarantee, and it's the one you can check cryptographically.

software · Wasm sandbox + process

You vs. other tenants

CC seals the whole GPU as a single trust domain and disables MIG, so there's no hardware partition between tenants on one card. We isolate tenants with two software layers, and each guards a different side. On the CPU, each tenant's app runs inside a WebAssembly sandbox (memory-safe, no ambient authority, no host filesystem or environment). On the GPU, the sandbox does nothing; kernel execution is separated by the dedicated OS worker process each tenant gets: separate processes get separate GPU address spaces, mediated by the shared NVIDIA driver. Those boundaries, not a hardware slice, are what keep tenants apart, and we don't pretend otherwise.

What the Wasm sandbox and process boundary actually buy you · every GPU-level point below was measured on our own hardware

  • No host reach. The Wasm app can't open files, read the host environment, spawn processes, or make outbound connections. WASI grants it only the HTTP socket the runtime serves it on. There's no shell and no SSH, anywhere: a Wasm app isn't an OS, so the usual container-escape surface simply isn't there, and the platform ships no SSH channel at all - no sshd runs in the enclave and nothing installs keys. The only ways in are the HTTP data path and the ports an app declares.
  • No cross-tenant reads. Separate processes get separate GPU address spaces. A kernel in one tenant's process that tries to read another tenant's memory faults. The address simply isn't mapped. (Verified: the cross-process read attempt raised an illegal-access fault.)
  • Blast radius of one. A tenant whose kernel crashes or runs illegal memory access takes down only their own worker. A co-tenant running on the same GPU kept executing without a single error, and the GPU stayed healthy. (Verified under a live fault.)
  • Memory zeroed before reuse. The driver hands VRAM pages to a process only zeroed, so one tenant's residue is never observable to the next. Full disclosure about what our test does and doesn't show: zero-on-allocation is stock driver behavior, so "a fresh allocation read back zeroes" wouldn't prove much on its own. What we verified is that the property holds in the case that matters: under CC, after a tenant wrote ~117 GB and was killed without cleaning up, another process's fresh allocation came back fully zeroed. Whether the driver scrubs at exit or on allocation is its implementation detail; dirty pages crossing tenants is the failure, and it didn't happen.
  • Fair share, not free-for-all. Each worker is capped to its purchased compute share so one tenant can't starve the GPU, and a watchdog kills any worker that exceeds its slice or hangs.

What we do not claim

Inter-tenant isolation is enforced by the WebAssembly sandbox, the operating system, and the NVIDIA GPU driver, the one component every tenant's app passes through to reach the card. So tenants are isolated from each other at the sandbox-and-process level, modulo the integrity of that shared driver, not by per-tenant hardware partitioning (which this GPU generation does not offer with confidential computing on). If your threat model requires hardware-enforced isolation from other tenants specifically, run a dedicated full-GPU deployment instead of a shared one. Isolation from us, the operator, is hardware-enforced and attested either way.

Pricing

Metered per second. Paid on-chain.

Deploying is two dials - what you rent - and two rates - what you pay. USDC or ETH up front buys runtime by the second; the instance runs until that time is used up, and you top up to extend it. Nothing is custodied - payments forward in the same transaction, to Enclave and, for apps that charge a publisher fee, that share straight to the publisher's wallet - and are final. Unspent time stays on the deployment's on-chain record (stop now, resume later) but isn't withdrawable, so fund in small top-ups.

GPU / VRAM dial × $6/hr per full card

0-100% of one GPU card. VRAM and compute move together: each percent buys ≈1.4 GB VRAM and ≈9.9 TFLOPS. 0% = a CPU-only app.

CPU / RAM dial × $3/hr per whole node

0-100% of the node's vCPU+RAM: each percent buys ≈0.16 vCPU, ≈0.64 GB RAM, ≈10 GFLOPS (a whole node is roughly a thousandth of a card's FLOPS). Your app's memory cap is exactly this slice of the node's 64 GB.

Floors come from app specs minimums, not suggestions

The VRAM, compute, and RAM an app declares in the catalog set each dial's minimum: spec ÷ server spec (140.4 GB / 989 TFLOPS per card; 64 GB / ~1000 GFLOPS per node), larger axis wins, rounded up to a whole percent. An app declaring 494 TFLOPS can't deploy below a 50% GPU share even if it needs 2 GB of VRAM - you can't occupy an axis without paying for it.

Rules of the road software caps · CC disables MIG

A GPU app's GPU share must be at least its CPU share (its CPU slice rides on the card's node) - so a 100%-GPU, 10%-CPU tenant leaves 90% of that node for CPU-only apps. Unsold CPU on a GPU node is never stranded. Shares are runtime-enforced software caps, not hardware MIG partitions.

contract

price = GPU% × $6/hr  +  CPU% × $3/hr  ·  metered per second

One honest rounding note: the per-second rate is settled in whole millionths of a USDC, rounded up - so the very smallest slices meter a hair above the formula (a 1%-CPU app runs at $0.0324/hr rather than $0.0300). Every estimate shown on the deploy pages already includes this. Some store apps also charge a small hourly publisher fee - capped on-chain, shown in the rate before you sign, and paid straight to the app publisher's wallet.

CPU-only0%10%no GPU · 6.4 GB RAM / 1.6 vCPU$0.30$0.000084
Slice10%5%~14 GB VRAM / ~99 TFLOPS · 3.2 GB RAM$0.75$0.000209
Quarter25%5%~35 GB VRAM / ~247 TFLOPS · 3.2 GB RAM$1.65$0.000459
Half50%10%~70 GB VRAM / ~495 TFLOPS · 6.4 GB RAM$3.30$0.000917
Full GPU100%10%140.4 GB VRAM / 989 TFLOPS · 6.4 GB RAM$6.30$0.001751

Example points on a continuous scale, not a fixed menu - the dials move in whole-percent steps. A Full GPU deployment (100%) is the option when you want no co-tenants on the card at all: it's the only configuration where isolation from other tenants is also hardware-enforced. On a shared card, tenants are isolated from each other at the sandbox-and-process level (how →) and always isolated from the operator by confidential computing.

The company

Who's behind this.

Enclave is designed so you never have to take the operator's word for anything - but you should still know whose word you're not taking. Enclave is built and operated by Enclave Host, Inc.

the operator

Enclave Host, Inc.

The company behind Enclave. Payments forward to it on-chain in the open, and its enclaves prove what they're running before you send them a byte - the whole point is that "trust us" never has to enter the conversation.

the founder

Steven Batchelor

Founder & CEO

Steven is a certified application architect focused on serverless architecture, with an extensive consulting background serving large enterprise clients and a passion for decentralized systems. He designs and builds Enclave hands-on: the attested enclave stack, the on-chain contracts, and the site you're reading. Based in Arizona.

blocks.html_en: • On boot the AMD secure processor signs a report over the launch measurement: a SHA-384 digest binding the firmware, the kernel, and the exact runtime image. Change one byte and it changes. Your app is deliberately not in this boot measurement: it arrives later by CID, and the measured runtime refuses bytes that don't hash to the CID in your on-chain deployment record. The attestation reports the app's CID and states exactly what covers it; it never pretends the boot quote does. (An Intel TDX host tells the same story via a DCAP quote over MRTD + RTMRs.)

blocks.html_en: Your browser loads Tinfoil's open-source verifier (@tinfoilsh/verifier) and fetches the attestation of one live enclave - the fleet's gateway nominates an active one, and the verdict names exactly which - over your own connection. It validates the hardware report up to the silicon vendor's root of trust (AMD SEV-SNP on today's fleet; the steps below name the technology the enclave actually presented), verifies the release's Sigstore code provenance against the public EnclaveHost/enclave repo, and compares the two measurements. Exactly what the API's verify field tells every client to do - and what your dashboard re-runs against the specific enclave each deployment lands on.

sentences.en: You vs. the operator

blocks.html_en: Your enclave runs in a CPU TEE (AMD SEV-SNP on today's fleet) with the GPU in NVIDIA confidential-computing mode. The CPU↔GPU link is encrypted and the GPU is walled off from the host. Enclave, the operator, cannot read your VRAM, RAM, keys, or traffic. This is enforced by hardware and proven by an attestation you verify in your browser before sending data. This is the strong guarantee, and it's the one you can check cryptographically.

sentences.zh: 这是强有力的保证，并且您可以通过密码进行检查。

sentences.en: software · Wasm sandbox + process
sentences.zh: 软件·Wasm沙箱+进程

sentences.en: You vs. other tenants
sentences.zh: 您与其他租户

blocks.html_en: CC seals the whole GPU as a single trust domain and disables MIG, so there's no hardware partition between tenants on one card. We isolate tenants with two software layers, and each guards a different side. On the CPU, each tenant's app runs inside a WebAssembly sandbox (memory-safe, no ambient authority, no host filesystem or environment). On the GPU, the sandbox does nothing; kernel execution is separated by the dedicated OS worker process each tenant gets: separate processes get separate GPU address spaces, mediated by the shared NVIDIA driver. Those boundaries, not a hardware slice, are what keep tenants apart, and we don't pretend otherwise.

sentences.en: CC seals the whole GPU as a single trust domain and disables MIG, so there's no hardware partition between tenants on one card.
sentences.zh: CC 将整个 GPU 密封为单个信任域并禁用 MIG，因此一张卡上的租户之间不存在硬件分区。

sentences.en: We isolate tenants with two software layers, and each guards a different side.
sentences.zh: 我们用两个软件层隔离租户，每个层保护不同的一面。

sentences.en: On the CPU, each tenant's app runs inside a WebAssembly sandbox (memory-safe, no ambient authority, no host filesystem or environment).

sentences.en: On the GPU, the sandbox does nothing; kernel execution is separated by the dedicated OS worker process each tenant gets: separate processes get separate GPU address spaces, mediated by the shared NVIDIA driver.
sentences.zh: 在 GPU 上，沙箱不执行任何操作；内核执行由每个租户获得的专用操作系统工作进程分开：单独的进程获得单独的 GPU 地址空间，由共享 NVIDIA 驱动程序协调。

sentences.en: Those boundaries, not a hardware slice, are what keep tenants apart, and we don't pretend otherwise.
sentences.zh: 这些界限，而不是硬件切片，才是将租户分开的原因，我们不会假装不是这样。

blocks.html_en: What the Wasm sandbox and process boundary actually buy you · every GPU-level point below was measured on our own hardware

sentences.en: What the Wasm sandbox and process boundary actually buy you · every GPU-level point below was measured on our own hardware
sentences.zh: Wasm 沙箱和进程边界实际上为您带来了什么 · 下面的每个 GPU 级别的点都是在我们自己的硬件上测量的

blocks.html_en: • No host reach. The Wasm app can't open files, read the host environment, spawn processes, or make outbound connections. WASI grants it only the HTTP socket the runtime serves it on. There's no shell and no SSH, anywhere: a Wasm app isn't an OS, so the usual container-escape surface simply isn't there, and the platform ships no SSH channel at all - no sshd runs in the enclave and nothing installs keys. The only ways in are the HTTP data path and the ports an app declares.

sentences.en: No host reach.
sentences.zh: 无法到达主机。

sentences.en: The Wasm app can't open files, read the host environment, spawn processes, or make outbound connections.
sentences.zh: Wasm 应用程序无法打开文件、读取主机环境、生成进程或建立出站连接。

sentences.en: WASI grants it only the HTTP socket the runtime serves it on.
sentences.zh: WASI 仅向其授予运行时为其提供服务的 HTTP 套接字。

sentences.en: There's no shell and no SSH, anywhere: a Wasm app isn't an OS, so the usual container-escape surface simply isn't there, and the platform ships no SSH channel at all - no sshd runs in the enclave and nothing installs keys.
sentences.zh: 任何地方都没有 shell，也没有 SSH：Wasm 应用程序不是操作系统，因此通常的容器逃逸表面根本不存在，并且该平台根本不提供 SSH 通道 - 飞地中没有运行 sshd，也没有安装密钥。

sentences.en: The only ways in are the HTTP data path and the ports an app declares.
sentences.zh: 唯一的途径是 HTTP 数据路径和应用程序声明的端口。

blocks.html_en: • No cross-tenant reads. Separate processes get separate GPU address spaces. A kernel in one tenant's process that tries to read another tenant's memory faults. The address simply isn't mapped. (Verified: the cross-process read attempt raised an illegal-access fault.)

sentences.en: No cross-tenant reads.
sentences.zh: 没有跨租户读取。

sentences.en: Separate processes get separate GPU address spaces.
sentences.zh: 单独的进程获得单独的 GPU 地址空间。

sentences.en: A kernel in one tenant's process that tries to read another tenant's memory faults.
sentences.zh: 一个租户进程中的内核尝试读取另一租户的内存错误。

sentences.en: The address simply isn't mapped.
sentences.zh: 该地址根本没有映射。

sentences.en: (Verified: the cross-process read attempt raised an illegal-access fault.)
sentences.zh: （已验证：跨进程读取尝试引发了非法访问错误。）

blocks.html_en: • Blast radius of one. A tenant whose kernel crashes or runs illegal memory access takes down only their own worker. A co-tenant running on the same GPU kept executing without a single error, and the GPU stayed healthy. (Verified under a live fault.)

sentences.en: Blast radius of one.
sentences.zh: 爆炸半径为一。

sentences.en: A tenant whose kernel crashes or runs illegal memory access takes down only their own worker.
sentences.zh: 内核崩溃或运行非法内存访问的租户只会关闭自己的工作线程。

sentences.en: A co-tenant running on the same GPU kept executing without a single error, and the GPU stayed healthy.
sentences.zh: 在同一 GPU 上运行的共同租户继续执行，没有出现任何错误，并且 GPU 保持正常运行。

sentences.en: (Verified under a live fault.)
sentences.zh: （在带电故障下进行验证。）

blocks.html_en: • Memory zeroed before reuse. The driver hands VRAM pages to a process only zeroed, so one tenant's residue is never observable to the next. Full disclosure about what our test does and doesn't show: zero-on-allocation is stock driver behavior, so "a fresh allocation read back zeroes" wouldn't prove much on its own. What we verified is that the property holds in the case that matters: under CC, after a tenant wrote ~117 GB and was killed without cleaning up, another process's fresh allocation came back fully zeroed. Whether the driver scrubs at exit or on allocation is its implementation detail; dirty pages crossing tenants is the failure, and it didn't happen.

sentences.en: Memory zeroed before reuse.
sentences.zh: 内存在重用之前归零。

sentences.en: The driver hands VRAM pages to a process only zeroed, so one tenant's residue is never observable to the next.
sentences.zh: 驱动程序将 VRAM 页面交给仅清零的进程，因此一个租户的剩余内容永远无法被下一个租户观察到。

sentences.en: Full disclosure about what our test does and doesn't show: zero-on-allocation is stock driver behavior, so "a fresh allocation read back zeroes" wouldn't prove much on its own.
sentences.zh: 全面披露我们的测试显示和未显示的内容：分配为零是股票驱动行为，因此“新分配读回零”本身并不能证明太多。

sentences.en: What we verified is that the property holds in the case that matters: under CC, after a tenant wrote ~117 GB and was killed without cleaning up, another process's fresh allocation came back fully zeroed.
sentences.zh: 我们验证的是，该属性在重要的情况下保持不变：在 CC 下，在租户写入约 117 GB 并在没有清理的情况下被杀死后，另一个进程的新分配完全归零。

sentences.en: Whether the driver scrubs at exit or on allocation is its implementation detail; dirty pages crossing tenants is the failure, and it didn't happen.
sentences.zh: 驱动程序是在退出时还是在分配时进行清理是其实现细节；跨租户的脏页就是失败，但它并没有发生。

blocks.html_en: • Fair share, not free-for-all. Each worker is capped to its purchased compute share so one tenant can't starve the GPU, and a watchdog kills any worker that exceeds its slice or hangs.

sentences.en: Fair share, not free-for-all.
sentences.zh: 公平分享，而不是免费。

sentences.en: Each worker is capped to its purchased compute share so one tenant can't starve the GPU, and a watchdog kills any worker that exceeds its slice or hangs.
sentences.zh: 每个工作线程都有其购买的计算份额的上限，因此一个租户不会让 GPU 挨饿，并且看门狗会杀死任何超出其切片或挂起的工作线程。

sentences.en: What we do not claim
sentences.zh: 我们不主张什么

blocks.html_en: Inter-tenant isolation is enforced by the WebAssembly sandbox, the operating system, and the NVIDIA GPU driver, the one component every tenant's app passes through to reach the card. So tenants are isolated from each other at the sandbox-and-process level, modulo the integrity of that shared driver, not by per-tenant hardware partitioning (which this GPU generation does not offer with confidential computing on). If your threat model requires hardware-enforced isolation from other tenants specifically, run a dedicated full-GPU deployment instead of a shared one. Isolation from us, the operator, is hardware-enforced and attested either way.

sentences.en: Inter-tenant isolation is enforced by the WebAssembly sandbox, the operating system, and the NVIDIA GPU driver, the one component every tenant's app passes through to reach the card.
sentences.zh: 租户间隔离由 WebAssembly 沙箱、操作系统和 NVIDIA GPU 驱动程序强制执行，这是每个租户的应用程序到达卡时都要经过的一个组件。

blocks.html_en: Deploying is two dials - what you rent - and two rates - what you pay. Nothing is custodied - payments forward in the same transaction, to Enclave and, for apps that charge a publisher fee, that share straight to the publisher's wallet - and are final. Unspent time stays on the deployment's on-chain record (stop now, resume later) but isn't withdrawable, so fund in small top-ups.

content_html_zh:
无需信任、经过验证的飞地 · API v1

过去隐藏在企业销售电话背后的硬件现在只需点击一下即可：直接从浏览器将 WebAssembly 应用程序部署到配备旗舰 NVIDIA GPU 的机密飞地上。 以 ETH 或 USDC 每秒支付。 你的钱包就是你的账户。 然后在发送单个字节的数据之前亲自检查加密证明。

旗舰 NVIDIA GPU · 每个租户的机密 CPU + GPU Wasm 沙箱 + 进程隔离 VRAM 在重用之前归零 CORS 友好

飞地 · sev-snp + nvidia-cc

发射测量·实时获取……

型号

不要相信运营商。 测量一下。

Enclave 在硬件隔离、经过验证的 enclave 中运行您的 WebAssembly 应用程序，并连接了机密 GPU，因此操作员无法读取您的内存、密钥或流量，并且您不必相信我们的话。 您可以在链上或在签名证明中检查此页面上的每项声明。 我们同样明确了非硬件强制的部分：租户如何在共享 GPU 上彼此隔离。 了解隔离的具体工作原理 ↓

可验证的

无需信任

每个 enclave 都会生成一份 CPU 认证报告（当今机群上的 AMD SEV-SNP）和 NVIDIA GPU 认证报告。 根据 EnclaveHost/enclave 版本上发布的测量结果在客户端验证它们，然后连接。

钱包原生

加密原生

你的钱包就是你的账户，没有任何东西被托管：每笔付款都在同一笔交易中转发到 Enclave，对于付费应用程序，发行商直接分享到他们的钱包；合约从不持有资金。 它购买预付费运行时，在部署运行时每秒消耗一次。 付款是最终的：未花费的时间在停止后仍然存在，但无法撤回，因此请先存入少量资金，然后再充值。

cors

浏览器原生

整个 API 均支持 CORS。 启动服务器、流式传输日志并从前端 JavaScript 提取证明。 无需后端。

gpu

真正的 GPU

整个旗舰 NVIDIA GPU 处于机密计算模式，通过 GPU 接口连接到您的 Wasm 应用程序。 部署是两个表盘 - 一个 GPU 份额和一个 CPU 份额 - 最低限度为应用程序声明的规格所暗示的。 租户如何共享卡 →

生命周期

五通电话，从头到尾。

从空钱包到经过验证的 GPU 端点的路径。 单击一个步骤即可准确查看穿过电线的内容。

信任之根

这里的“不信任”实际上意味着什么。

“不信任”不是一句口号。 这是一系列测量结果，最终在您的浏览器中结束。 浏览每个链接以查看已验证的内容以及操作员无法伪造的原因。
  1. CPU（当今机群中的 AMD SEV-SNP）和 NVIDIA GPU 的密钥在制造时已融合。 Enclave 无法提取或伪造它们。 芯片供应商（AMD 或 NVIDIA、TDX 主机上的英特尔）是信任的根源，而不是运营商。
  2. 启动时，AMD 安全处理器会签署一份关于启动测量的报告：绑定固件、内核和确切运行时映像的 SHA-384 摘要。 改变一个字节，它就会改变。 您的应用程序故意不在此启动测量中：它稍后通过 CID 到达，并且测量的运行时间拒绝未散列到链上部署记录中的 CID 的字节。 该证明报告了应用程序的 CID 并准确说明了其涵盖的内容；它从不假装引导引用是这样的。 （英特尔 TDX 主机通过 MRTD + RTMR 上的 DCAP 引用讲述了同样的故事。）
  3. 启用机密计算后，GPU 会根据您的随机数签署一份证明报告，在任何数据到达 VRAM 之前证明 CC 模式、驱动程序和 vBIOS。 对照 NVIDIA 的 NRAS 或 nvtrust 进行检查。
  4. enclave 在密封内存中生成其 TLS 密钥对，并将公钥哈希折叠到报价的 report_data 中。 私钥永远不会离开边界，因此操作者不能坐在中间。
  5. Tinfoil 的开源验证器在客户端运行整个链：针对供应商根的硬件报告、版本的 Sigstore 来源、测量比较、TLS 绑定。 API 的 verify.selfCheck 是在自身上运行相同步骤的飞地：标记的诊断，因为信任仅在您的客户端计算时才计算在内。 下面现场尝试一下。

获取 /v1/deployments/{id}/attestation

不要相信此页面的说法：立即运行检查

您的浏览器加载 Tinfoil 的开源验证器 (@tinfoilsh/verifier) 并获取一个实时飞地的证明 - 舰队的网关指定一个活跃的飞地，并且判决准确地命名哪个飞地 - 通过您自己的连接。 它根据芯片供应商的信任根（当今机群上的 AMD SEV-SNP；以下步骤命名了 enclave 实际提供的技术）验证硬件报告，根据公共 EnclaveHost/enclave 存储库验证版本的 Sigstore 代码来源，并比较两个测量值。 API 的验证字段确切地告诉每个客户端要做的事情 - 以及您的仪表板针对每个部署所登陆的特定 enclave 重新运行的内容。

验证器模块由该站点本身提供（同源、版本固定），而不是第三方 CDN；它检查的 Sigstore 来源捆绑包和芯片供应商的证书抵押品 (AMD KDS) 来自 Tinfoil 的 CORS 代理。 完全独立的路径：tinfoil attestation verify -e <enclave-host> -r EnclaveHost/enclave，通过 tinfoil-cli - 交换任何 enclave 的主机，以使机群的其余部分通过相同的检查。

如何实现隔离

两个界限。 一是硬件。 一个不是。 我们告诉你是哪一个。

大多数“机密 GPU”宣传都将这些内容模糊在一起。 Enclave 将它们分开，因为它们由不同的事物强制执行并具有不同的保证。 这正是保护您以及免受谁侵害的因素。

硬件·经过认证

您与操作员

您的 enclave 在 CPU TEE（当今机群中的 AMD SEV-SNP）中运行，GPU 处于 NVIDIA 机密计算模式。 CPU↔GPU 链接已加密，GPU 与主机隔离。 Enclave 操作员无法读取您的 VRAM、RAM、密钥或流量。 这是由硬件强制执行的，并由您在发送数据之前在浏览器中验证的证明来证明。 这是强有力的保证，并且您可以通过密码进行检查。

软件·Wasm沙箱+进程

您与其他租户

CC 将整个 GPU 密封为单个信任域并禁用 MIG，因此一张卡上的租户之间不存在硬件分区。 我们用两个软件层隔离租户，每个层保护不同的一面。 在 CPU 上，每个租户的应用程序在 WebAssembly 沙箱内运行（内存安全、没有环境权限、没有主机文件系统或环境）。 在 GPU 上，沙箱不执行任何操作；内核执行由每个租户获得的专用操作系统工作进程分开：单独的进程获得单独的 GPU 地址空间，由共享 NVIDIA 驱动程序协调。 这些界限，而不是硬件切片，才是将租户分开的原因，我们不会假装不是这样。

Wasm 沙箱和进程边界实际上为您带来了什么 · 下面的每个 GPU 级别的点都是在我们自己的硬件上测量的

  • 无法到达主机。 Wasm 应用程序无法打开文件、读取主机环境、生成进程或建立出站连接。 WASI 仅向其授予运行时为其提供服务的 HTTP 套接字。 任何地方都没有 shell，也没有 SSH：Wasm 应用程序不是操作系统，因此通常的容器逃逸表面根本不存在，并且该平台根本不提供 SSH 通道 - 飞地中没有运行 sshd，也没有安装密钥。 唯一的途径是 HTTP 数据路径和应用程序声明的端口。
  • 没有跨租户读取。 单独的进程获得单独的 GPU 地址空间。 一个租户进程中的内核尝试读取另一租户的内存错误。 该地址根本没有映射。 （已验证：跨进程读取尝试引发了非法访问错误。）
  • 爆炸半径为一。 内核崩溃或运行非法内存访问的租户只会关闭自己的工作线程。 在同一 GPU 上运行的共同租户继续执行，没有出现任何错误，并且 GPU 保持正常运行。 （在带电故障下进行验证。）
  • 内存在重用之前归零。 驱动程序将 VRAM 页面交给仅清零的进程，因此一个租户的剩余内容永远无法被下一个租户观察到。 全面披露我们的测试显示和未显示的内容：分配为零是股票驱动行为，因此“新分配读回零”本身并不能证明太多。 我们验证的是，该属性在重要的情况下保持不变：在 CC 下，在租户写入约 117 GB 并在没有清理的情况下被杀死后，另一个进程的新分配完全归零。 驱动程序是在退出时还是在分配时进行清理是其实现细节；跨租户的脏页就是失败，但它并没有发生。
  • 公平分享，而不是免费。 每个工作线程都有其购买的计算份额的上限，因此一个租户不会让 GPU 挨饿，并且看门狗会杀死任何超出其切片或挂起的工作线程。

我们不主张什么

租户间隔离由 WebAssembly 沙箱、操作系统和 NVIDIA GPU 驱动程序强制执行，这是每个租户的应用程序到达卡时都要经过的一个组件。 因此，租户在沙箱和进程级别彼此隔离，以共享驱动程序的完整性为模，而不是通过每个租户的硬件分区（这一代 GPU 不提供机密计算）。 如果您的威胁模型需要与其他租户进行硬件强制隔离，请运行专用的全 GPU 部署，而不是共享部署。 与我们（操作员）的隔离是由硬件强制执行的，并且无论哪种方式都得到了证明。

CPU / RAM 拨号 × 每个整个节点 3 美元/小时

节点的 vCPU+RAM 的 0-100%：每个百分比购买约 0.16 个 vCPU、约 0.64 GB RAM、约 10 GFLOPS（整个节点大约是卡的 FLOPS 的千分之一）。 您的应用程序的内存上限正是节点 64 GB 的这一部分。

楼层来自应用程序最低规格，而不是建议

应用程序在目录中声明的 VRAM、计算和 RAM 设置每个表盘的最小值：规格 ÷ 服务器规格（每卡 140.4 GB / 989 TFLOPS；每节点 64 GB / ~1000 GFLOPS），较大的轴获胜，四舍五入到整数百分比。 声明 494 TFLOPS 的应用程序无法部署低于 50% 的 GPU 共享，即使它需要 2 GB VRAM - 您无法在不付费的情况下占用一个轴。

道路软件上限规则 · CC 禁用 MIG

GPU 应用程序的 GPU 份额必须至少是其 CPU 份额（其 CPU 切片位于卡的节点上） - 因此 100%-GPU、10%-CPU 租户将该节点的 90% 留给仅 CPU 应用程序。 GPU 节点上未售出的 CPU 永远不会闲置。 共享是运行时强制的软件上限，而不是硬件 MIG 分区。
